label: chapter1liang12e.json
title: Introduction to Computers, Programs, and Java

questions.text: ________ is the physical aspect of the computer that can be seen.
questions.explanation: Hardware refers to the physical components of a computer system, such as the motherboard, monitor, keyboard, and mouse, which can be seen and touched.

questions.text: __________ is the brain of a computer.
questions.explanation: The CPU (Central Processing Unit) is called the 'brain' because it interprets and executes instructions from software and coordinates the activities of other hardware components.

questions.text: The speed of the CPU may be measured in __________. (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: CPU speed is measured by its clock frequency in hertz. Common units include megahertz (MHz) and gigahertz (GHz). Megabytes and gigabytes are units of data storage capacity, not speed.

questions.text: Why do computers use zeros and ones?

choices.label: A
choices.text: because combinations of zeros and ones can represent any numbers and characters.

choices.label: B
choices.text: because digital devices have two stable states and it is natural to use one state for 0 and the other for 1.

choices.label: D
choices.text: because binary numbers are the bases upon which all other number systems are built.

questions.explanation: Electronic components like transistors naturally have two states (on/off, or high/low voltage). Representing these states with the binary digits 0 and 1 is the most efficient and reliable way to design computer circuitry.

questions.text: One byte has ________ bits.
questions.explanation: A byte is a fundamental unit of data in computing, and by standard convention, it consists of exactly 8 bits.

questions.text: One gigabyte is approximately ________ bytes.
questions.explanation: In the metric system (SI), the prefix 'giga-' stands for 10^9, which is 1,000,000,000 (one billion). In binary terms, it is 2^30, which is slightly more than 1 billion.

questions.text: A program and its data must be moved into the computer's _______ before they can be executed by the CPU.
questions.explanation: The CPU can only execute instructions that are loaded into the computer's RAM (Random Access Memory). Programs stored on permanent storage like a hard disk must be copied to memory first.

questions.text: A computer's _______ is volatile; that is, any information stored in it is lost when the system's power is turned off.
questions.explanation: Volatile memory (like RAM) requires electricity to maintain the stored information. Hard disks, flash sticks, and CD-ROMs are non-volatile, meaning they retain data even without power.

questions.text: Which of the following are storage devices? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: All the options listed are examples of secondary storage devices used to store data for long periods, unlike primary memory (RAM) which is used for temporary processing.

questions.text: The ____________ specifies the number of pixels in horizontal and vertical dimensions of the display device.
questions.explanation: Screen resolution is the measure of how many pixels are displayed across the width and height of a screen (e.g., 1920 by 1080).

questions.text: ____________ is a device to connect a computer to a local area network (LAN).
questions.explanation: A Network Interface Card (NIC) is the hardware component that allows a computer to connect to a local area network (LAN). Modems (Regular, DSL, or Cable) are typically used for connecting to a Wide Area Network (WAN) or the Internet.

questions.text: ____________ are instructions to the computer. Please select all that apply.
questions.explanation: Software and Programs refer to the sets of instructions that tell a computer's hardware what to do. Hardware and keyboards are the physical components themselves.

questions.text: Computer can execute the code in ____________.
questions.explanation: Machine language is the low-level binary code (0s and 1s) that a computer's CPU can understand and execute directly. Assembly and high-level languages must be translated before execution.

questions.text: ___________ translates high-level language program into machine language program.
questions.explanation: A compiler is a specialized program that translates source code written in a high-level language into machine language or bytecode so it can be executed by the computer.

questions.text: ____________ is an operating system.
questions.explanation: Windows is a software platform that manages computer hardware and software resources. Java, C++, Visual Basic, and Ada are all programming languages.

questions.text: _____________ is a program that runs on a computer to manage and control a computer's activities.
questions.explanation: The Operating System (OS) acts as the intermediary between the user and the hardware, managing memory, processes, and all other system operations.

questions.text: Due to security reasons, Java ___________ cannot run from a Web browser in the new version of Java.
questions.explanation: Java applets were small applications that ran inside web browsers. They were deprecated and eventually removed from modern versions of Java due to their history of security vulnerabilities.

questions.text: ________ is not an object-oriented programming language.
questions.explanation: C is a procedural programming language. Java, C++, C#, and Python are all languages that support object-oriented programming (OOP) principles.

questions.text: ________ is interpreted.
questions.explanation: Java is often called an interpreted language because its source code is compiled into bytecode, which is then interpreted by the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) at runtime.

questions.text: ________ is architecture-neutral.
questions.explanation: Java is architecture-neutral because it compiles to bytecode that can run on any hardware platform that has a compatible Java Virtual Machine (JVM).

questions.text: ________ is a technical definition of the language that includes the syntax and semantics of the Java programming language.
questions.explanation: The Java Language Specification (JLS) is the official technical document that defines the syntax, grammar, and semantics of the Java language.

questions.text: ________ contains predefined classes and interfaces for developing Java programs.
questions.explanation: The Java Application Programming Interface (API), also known as the Java library, contains thousands of predefined classes and interfaces for building applications.

questions.text: ________ consists of a set of separate programs for developing and testing Java programs, each of which is invoked from a command line.
questions.explanation: The Java Development Kit (JDK) is a set of command-line tools (such as javac for compiling and java for running) used for developing and testing Java software.

questions.text: ________ provides an integrated development environment (IDE) for rapidly developing Java programs. Editing, compiling, building, debugging, and online help are integrated in one graphical user interface.
questions.explanation: A Java Integrated Development Environment (IDE), such as Eclipse or IntelliJ IDEA, bundles multiple development tools into a single software application with a GUI.

questions.text: The main method header is written as:
questions.explanation: In Java, the main method must be `public`, `static`, and `void`. Because Java is case-sensitive, `String` must be capitalized (as it is a class) and `main` must be lowercase.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Every line in a program must end with a semicolon.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Every statement in a program must end with a semicolon.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Every comment line must end with a semicolon.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Every method must end with a semicolon.

choices.label: E
choices.text: Every class must end with a semicolon.

questions.explanation: Java syntax requires that every executable statement ends with a semicolon (`;`). Comments, method signatures, and class definitions do not follow this rule.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is correct to display Welcome to Java on the console?
questions.explanation: In Java, String literals must be enclosed in double quotes (`" "`). Single quotes (`' '`) are used exclusively for single character literals (`char`).
The first statement prints the string literal "1 + 2 + 3" exactly as it appears. The second statement evaluates the numerical addition of 1, 2, and 3, which results in the integer 6.

questions.text: The JDK command to just compile a class (not run) in the file `Test.java` is:
questions.explanation: To compile a Java source file from the command line, you use the `javac` command followed by the filename including its `.java` extension.

questions.text: Which JDK command is correct to run a Java application in `ByteCode.class`?
questions.explanation: To run a compiled Java class, you use the `java` command followed by the class name. Do not include the `.class` file extension in this command.

questions.text: Java compiler translates Java source code into _________.
questions.explanation: The Java compiler (javac) converts high-level Java source code into an intermediate, platform-independent format called bytecode.

questions.text: _________ is a software that interprets Java bytecode.
questions.explanation: The Java Virtual Machine (JVM) is responsible for loading, verifying, and executing (interpreting or JIT-compiling) Java bytecode on a specific hardware platform.
In Java, a public class must be saved in a source file that has the exact same name as the class, including case-sensitivity, with the `.java` extension.

questions.text: The extension name of a Java bytecode file is
questions.explanation: When source code is compiled, the resulting bytecode is stored in a file with the `.class` extension.

questions.text: The extension name of a Java source code file is
questions.explanation: Java source code files are plain text files created by developers and must use the `.java` extension.

questions.text: Which of the following lines is not a Java comment? Please select all that apply.
questions.explanation: Java supports single-line comments (`//`), multi-line comments (`/* */`), and Javadoc comments (`/** */`). The sequences `--` and `**` are not recognized as comment indicators in Java.

questions.text: Which of the following are the reserved words? Please select all that apply.
questions.explanation: Reserved words (keywords) are words that have a predefined meaning in the Java language. `public`, `static`, `void`, and `class` are all part of the Java keyword list.

questions.text: Every statement in Java ends with ________.
questions.explanation: Java uses the semicolon (`;`) as a statement terminator to signal the end of an instruction.

questions.text: A block is enclosed inside __________.
questions.explanation: In Java, a block of code is defined by a pair of curly braces `{ }`.

questions.text: Programming style is important, because ______________. Please select all that apply.

choices.label: A
choices.text: a program may not compile if it has a bad style

choices.label: B
choices.text: good programming style can make a program run faster

choices.label: C
choices.text: good programming style makes a program more readable

choices.label: D
choices.text: good programming style helps reduce programming errors

questions.explanation: While programming style (like indentation and spacing) does not affect compilation or execution speed, it is vital for human readability and helps developers avoid and find logic errors.

choices.label: A
choices.text: Both I and II can compile and run and display Welcome to Java, but the code in II has a better style than I.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Only the code in I can compile and run and display Welcome to Java.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only the code in II can compile and run and display Welcome to Java.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Both I and II can compile and run and display Welcome to Java, but the code in I has a better style than II.

questions.explanation: Java is whitespace-insensitive, meaning both code snippets are syntactically correct and will compile and run. However, Code I follows standard indentation practices, making it much easier to read and maintain than the condensed style of Code II.
Code snippet IV follows the consistent application of nested indentation. Each block level (class and then method) is shifted to the right, which clearly communicates the hierarchy of the code.

questions.text: If a program compiles fine, but it produces an incorrect result, then the program suffers __________.
questions.explanation: Logic errors occur when the syntax is correct (it compiles) and the program executes without crashing (no runtime error), but the output is wrong because the underlying algorithm or logic is flawed.

The code contains a syntax error because the string literal `"Welcome to Java!` is missing its closing double quote. Syntax violations prevent the compiler from generating bytecode, resulting in a compile error.

Java is case-sensitive. The correct class name for a string is `String` with a capital 'S'. Since `string` (lowercase) is not a defined type in Java, the compiler will fail to recognize it and produce a compile error.
